steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must name the poet (Emily Hiestand) and then attach a second descriptor (author of The Very Rich Hours). The question is about comma placement: which choice correctly separates the name from the following appositive phrase?

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — poet, Emily Hiestand,: Setting the name off with commas implies it's extra, nonessential information, but the name is identifying who the poet is — no commas should surround it. ✗
  • Choice B — poet, Emily Hiestand: A comma before the name breaks the essential 'role + name' unit. The name directly follows the role without punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — poet Emily Hiestand: Without a comma before 'author of...', the second descriptor runs into the name without the required separation for an appositive phrase. ✗
  • Choice D — poet Emily Hiestand,: No commas around the name (it identifies who the poet is), and one comma before 'author of...' correctly sets off the nonessential appositive. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
